quotes.text: the Company
our board of directors
the "Board Representatives"
BofA Merrill Lynch
King & Spalding LLP ("King & Spalding"), legal counsel to the Company
New Mountain Capital
Party X
Party Y
first discussed the possibility of exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.
unanimously agreed to proceed with certain initial steps related to exploring our strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.
it was not an appropriate time to explore strategic alternatives or engage a financial advisor
it was timely to revisit the previously discussed exploration of strategic alternatives.
BofA Merrill Lynch to act as our financial advisor in connection with reviewing our strategic alternatives.

unanimously approved moving forward with next steps, including contacting the potential buyers on the list discussed by our board of directors, executing confidentiality agreements with such parties and conducting meetings with such parties.
contacted fifty potential buyers (comprising twenty-eight strategic buyers and twenty-two financial buyers
we executed confidentiality agreements with a number of the potential buyers.
Each of the confidentiality agreements included employee non-solicitation provisions and a customary "standstill" provision, including a "don't ask to waive" provision.
We entered into a confidentiality agreement with New Mountain Capital on March 19, 2014.
distributed a first round process letter
non-binding preliminary indications of interest would be due no later than April 14, 2014.
twenty-five potential buyers informed us that they were not interested in pursuing a potential transaction and declined to enter into a confidentiality agreement with us
twenty-five potential buyers executed confidentiality agreements with us, received a copy of the marketing materials and/or met with the Company's management.
While New Mountain Capital received the marketing materials and the first round process letter, it decided at the time not to submit a preliminary indication of interest.
five parties, comprising four financial buyers and one strategic buyer, submitted preliminary and non-binding indications of interest.
The bids received in the preliminary indications of interest ranged from $20.00 per share to $22.00 per share.
determined to continue the process with the parties who had submitted preliminary indications of interest.
Party X submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest to BofA Merrill Lynch, increasing the number of preliminary indications of interest received by us as of such date to six.
The bid submitted by Party X ranged from $21.50 to $23.00 per share.
Party X informed representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that it was no longer interested in pursuing a potential transaction with the Company.
Party Y submitted a preliminary and non-binding indication of interest, bringing the total number of preliminary indications of interest received by us to seven.
The indication of interest submitted by Party Y ranged from $19.50 to $20.50 per share.
a draft of the merger agreement was distributed to the six remaining bidders (Party X having withdrawn from the process on May 14, 2014) that had submitted preliminary indications of interest.

five of the remaining six interested parties communicated to representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch that they were unable to proceed with the process due to concerns regarding valuation and, in some cases, the interested parties' own internal initiatives and strategic priorities.
The sixth remaining interested party declined to respond to BofA Merrill Lynch's communications regarding a potential transaction.

each of the seven parties that submitted a preliminary indication of interest had expressed concerns regarding valuation and believed that they could not pay the prices indicated in their preliminary indications of interest.
decided to terminate the process to explore potential strategic alternatives at that time
authorized letters to be delivered to those parties waiving the "don't ask to waive" provisions in their respective confidentiality agreements
expressed its interest in discussions with the Company regarding a potential transaction.
delivered an unsolicited indication of interest to the Company to acquire us for a per share price of $19.25.

supported by a highly confident financing letter from Jefferies Finance LLC, contained a request for a forty-five day exclusivity period and contemplated a "go-shop" period following the signing of any definitive merger agreement.

communicated to New Mountain Capital that our board of directors might be willing to consider granting New Mountain Capital a thirty day exclusivity period if New Mountain Capital raised its offer price and confirmed that a "go-shop" provision would be included in any potential definitive merger agreement.
delivered a revised indication of interest reflecting an increased per share price of $20.05, and indicated that this was the highest price it was willing to offer.
determined to move forward with negotiations with New Mountain Capital regarding the proposed sale of the Company.

we signed an agreement with New Mountain Capital extending the term of the confidentiality provision, the "standstill" provision and the employee non-solicitation provision in our original confidentiality agreement with them as well as providing for an exclusivity period with New Mountain Capital through March 31, 2015.
the $20.05 per share offer price was New Mountain Capital's "best and final" offer.
authorized an extension of exclusivity with New Mountain Capital through April 7, 2015, which extension letter was signed on March 18, 2015.
a draft merger agreement was delivered to New Mountain Capital and its advisors.
The draft merger agreement included, among other terms, a "go-shop" provision permitting us to solicit alternative acquisition proposals for sixty days after signing the merger agreement
BofA Merrill Lynch delivered to our board of directors an oral opinion, which was confirmed by delivery of a written opinion dated April 7, 2015

our board of directors unanimously determined that the merger agreement and the transactions contemplated thereby, including the merger, were advisable and in the best interests of our stockholders and the Company
the parties executed the merger agreement and the related transaction documents
issued a press release announcing the transaction on the morning of April 8, 2015.
commenced the go-shop process on our behalf on April 8, 2015.
contacted a total of fifty-eight parties (including potential strategic and financial buyers)
none of the parties contacted during the go-shop process had submitted a competing acquisition proposal to us
no such party remained engaged in discussions or negotiations with us or our representatives with respect to a potential acquisition proposal.
One of the financial buyers contacted during such time was New Mountain Capital.

cohorts.label: Fifty potential buyers
cohorts.membership_basis: Potential buyers contacted by BofA Merrill Lynch during the early-2014 outreach process.

cohorts.label: Twenty-eight strategic buyers
cohorts.membership_basis: Strategic buyers included in the fifty potential buyers contacted during the early-2014 outreach process.

cohorts.label: Twenty-two financial buyers
cohorts.membership_basis: Financial buyers included in the fifty potential buyers contacted during the early-2014 outreach process.

cohorts.label: Twenty-five engaged potential buyers
cohorts.membership_basis: Potential buyers that had executed confidentiality agreements, received marketing materials and/or met with management by March 27, 2014.

cohorts.label: Twenty-five uninterested potential buyers
cohorts.membership_basis: Potential buyers that informed the Company they were not interested and declined to enter confidentiality agreements by March 27, 2014.

cohorts.label: Five initial preliminary IOI submitters
cohorts.membership_basis: Parties that submitted preliminary non-binding indications of interest on April 14, 2014.

cohorts.label: Seven preliminary IOI submitters
cohorts.membership_basis: Parties that had submitted preliminary indications of interest by May 20, 2014.

cohorts.label: Six remaining bidders
cohorts.membership_basis: Bidders that remained after Party X withdrew from the seven preliminary IOI submitters.

cohorts.label: Five remaining bidders unable to proceed
cohorts.membership_basis: Five of the remaining six interested parties that said they were unable to proceed during the weeks after May 22, 2014.

cohorts.label: One remaining nonresponsive bidder
cohorts.membership_basis: The sixth remaining interested party that stopped responding during the weeks after May 22, 2014.

cohorts.label: Fifty-eight go-shop contacts
cohorts.membership_basis: Parties contacted by BofA Merrill Lynch during the go-shop process that began on April 8, 2015.

observations.summary: At a June 20, 2013 meeting, the board first discussed exploring strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.
On July 1, 2013, the board agreed to proceed with initial steps to explore strategic alternatives, including a potential sale of the Company.
On August 20, 2013, the board concluded that it was not an appropriate time to explore strategic alternatives or engage a financial advisor.
observations.other_detail: Board paused exploration of strategic alternatives and advisor engagement.

observations.summary: On January 7 and January 8, 2014, the board concluded that it was timely to revisit the exploration of strategic alternatives.
On January 28, 2014, the board approved an engagement letter for BofA Merrill Lynch to act as financial advisor in reviewing strategic alternatives.
On February 27, 2014, the board approved moving forward with contacting buyers, executing confidentiality agreements, and conducting meetings.

date.resolution_note: Anchored to the February 27, 2014 board approval and the March 27, 2014 process-letter update.

observations.summary: During the next several weeks after February 27, 2014, BofA Merrill Lynch contacted fifty potential buyers, comprising twenty-eight strategic buyers and twenty-two financial buyers.
observations.other_detail: Buyer outreach to potential strategic and financial acquirers.

date.resolution_note: The filing states the cumulative count of signed confidentiality agreements in the March 27, 2014 process update.

observations.summary: By March 27, 2014, twenty-five potential buyers had executed confidentiality agreements with the Company; those agreements included employee non-solicitation and standstill provisions, including a "don't ask to waive" provision.
On March 19, 2014, the Company entered into a confidentiality agreement with New Mountain Capital.
On March 27, 2014, BofA Merrill Lynch distributed a first-round process letter asking for preliminary non-binding indications of interest by April 14, 2014.

date.resolution_note: The filing states the cumulative count of uninterested buyers in the March 27, 2014 process update.

observations.summary: By March 27, 2014, twenty-five potential buyers had informed the Company they were not interested in pursuing a transaction and had declined to enter confidentiality agreements.
By March 27, 2014, New Mountain Capital had received the marketing materials and first-round process letter but decided not to submit a preliminary indication of interest.
observations.other_detail: Did not submit a preliminary indication of interest.

observations.summary: On April 14, 2014, five parties, comprising four financial buyers and one strategic buyer, submitted preliminary non-binding indications of interest ranging from $20.00 to $22.00 per share.
On April 16, 2014, the board decided to continue the process with the parties that had submitted preliminary indications of interest.
On May 9, 2014, Party X submitted a preliminary non-binding indication of interest ranging from $21.50 to $23.00 per share.
On May 14, 2014, Party X withdrew from the process before receiving data-room access or meeting management.
On May 20, 2014, Party Y submitted a preliminary non-binding indication of interest ranging from $19.50 to $20.50 per share.
On May 22, 2014, a draft merger agreement was distributed to the six remaining bidders that had submitted preliminary indications of interest.
observations.other_detail: Draft merger agreement distributed to remaining bidders.

date.resolution_note: Anchored to the May 22, 2014 draft merger agreement distribution and the June 19, 2014 board representatives meeting.

observations.summary: Over the next few weeks after May 22, 2014, five of the remaining six interested parties said they were unable to proceed because of valuation concerns and, in some cases, internal initiatives and strategic priorities.
observations.other_detail: Unable to proceed because of valuation concerns and, in some cases, internal initiatives and strategic priorities.

date.resolution_note: Anchored to the May 22, 2014 draft merger agreement distribution and the June 19, 2014 board representatives meeting.

observations.summary: Over the next few weeks after May 22, 2014, the sixth remaining interested party declined to respond to BofA Merrill Lynch's communications regarding a potential transaction.
observations.other_detail: Declined to respond to communications regarding a potential transaction.

observations.summary: On June 19, 2014, the board representatives were informed that each of the seven parties that had submitted preliminary indications of interest had valuation concerns and believed they could not pay the prices in those indications.
observations.other_detail: Each of the seven preliminary IOI submitters expressed valuation concerns and believed it could not pay the price in its indication.

observations.summary: On June 26, 2014, the board terminated the process to explore strategic alternatives.

date.raw_text: following the termination of the process
date.resolution_note: The waiver letters were authorized after the June 26, 2014 termination of the 2014 sale process.

observations.summary: Following termination of the 2014 process, the board authorized letters waiving the "don't ask to waive" provisions in the confidentiality agreements of parties that had signed them.
observations.other_detail: Waived the "don't ask to waive" provisions in existing confidentiality agreements.

observations.summary: On February 19, 2015, New Mountain Capital delivered an unsolicited indication of interest at $19.25 per share.
observations.other_detail: Supported by a highly confident financing letter from Jefferies Finance LLC, requested a forty-five day exclusivity period, and contemplated a go-shop provision.

observations.summary: On February 24, 2015, BofA Merrill Lynch told New Mountain Capital that the board might consider thirty days of exclusivity if New Mountain Capital raised its offer price and confirmed a go-shop provision.
observations.other_detail: Requested a raised indication of interest and confirmation of a go-shop provision as conditions for considering thirty days of exclusivity.

observations.summary: On February 26, 2015, New Mountain Capital delivered a revised indication of interest at $20.05 per share and said that was its highest price.
observations.other_detail: Again requested a forty-five day exclusivity period and reiterated willingness to accept a go-shop provision.

observations.summary: On February 26, 2015, the board determined to move forward with negotiations with New Mountain Capital regarding a sale of the Company.

On February 27, 2015, the Company signed an agreement with New Mountain Capital extending confidentiality, standstill, and employee non-solicitation terms and providing exclusivity through March 31, 2015.
observations.other_detail: Extended confidentiality, standstill, and employee non-solicitation provisions in the original confidentiality agreement.

observations.summary: On March 13, 2015, New Mountain Capital said its $20.05 per share offer was its best and final offer.
On March 18, 2015, the Company signed an extension of exclusivity with New Mountain Capital through April 7, 2015.
On March 18, 2015, a draft merger agreement was delivered to New Mountain Capital and its advisors.
observations.other_detail: Draft included a go-shop provision permitting solicitation of alternative acquisition proposals for sixty days after signing.

observations.summary: On April 7, 2015, BofA Merrill Lynch delivered an oral fairness opinion to the board, confirmed by written opinion dated April 7, 2015.
On April 7, 2015, the board determined that the merger agreement and related transactions were advisable and resolved to recommend adoption of the merger agreement.
observations.other_detail: Board approval and recommendation of merger agreement.

observations.summary: On April 8, 2015, the parties executed the merger agreement and related transaction documents.
On April 8, 2015, New Mountain Capital and the Company executed the merger agreement.
On the morning of April 8, 2015, the parties issued a press release announcing the transaction.
On April 8, 2015, BofA Merrill Lynch commenced the go-shop process on behalf of the Company and contacted fifty-eight parties regarding interest in a transaction.
observations.other_detail: Go-shop solicitation of alternative acquisition proposals under the merger agreement.

observations.summary: As of May 7, 2015, no party contacted during the go-shop process had submitted a competing acquisition proposal, and no party remained engaged.
observations.other_detail: No competing acquisition proposal was submitted and no contacted party remained engaged by the end of the go-shop period.
